description_long: This object is a vintage wooden television set, featuring a rectangular body with a natural wood finish and four slender, tapered legs. The front panel displays a large convex glass screen, flanked on one side by a fabric-covered speaker grille and below by three rotary control knobs and two push buttons. Two long, thin metal antennas extend from the top, angled outward for signal reception. The back panel is perforated for ventilation and includes a warning label, as well as a protruding black dome likely housing the cathode ray tube. The overall design is boxy and utilitarian, with visible screws and hardware, evoking mid-20th-century aesthetics. This television would typically be found in residential or retro-themed urban environments.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a convex glass screen, fabric speaker grille, rotary knobs, and dual metal antennas, standing on four tapered legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, fabric speaker grille, three rotary knobs, two push buttons, and both antennas.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden side panel, the angled antennas, and the depth of the television body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the perforated back panel, warning label, and a protruding black dome for the cathode ray tube.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain wooden panel and the angled antennas, with the legs visible.
attribute_bezel_design: medium-thick wooden bezel, natural brown color, rectangular with rounded corners, classic mid-century style
attribute_control_button_layout: three rotary knobs and two push buttons arranged in a row below the screen on the front panel
